response: convert the art collection tax by applying the 5% discount to the 20% rate, resulting in a 19% effective rate on the $2 million value, which equals $380,000. convert the original real estate tax by calculating 10% on the first $1 million, 15% on the next $2 million, and 20% on the remaining $2 million, totaling $900,000. convert the securities portfolio tax by subtracting the $100,000 charitable deduction from the $3 million value before applying the 15% rate, yielding $435,000. convert the initial total liability by summing the three asset taxes to reach $1,715,000. convert the real estate valuation by increasing the $5 million figure by 10% to reflect the actual market value of $5.5 million. convert the new real estate tax by applying the progressive rates to the adjusted value, charging 10% on the first $1 million, 15% on the next $2 million, and 20% on the remaining $2.5 million, which results in $1,000,000. convert the final total liability by adding the art tax of $380,000, the new real estate tax of $1,000,000, and the securities tax of $435,000 to arrive at $1,815,000.
